role_delineation_check: The Social Worker is responsible for selecting the home health agency and resolving the intake freeze, while the Navigator focuses on auditing demographic data in Atlantis, educating the family on the Healing Partners program, and flagging transition barriers without contacting the vendor directly.
narrative_summary: Elena Rodriguez, a sixty-nine-year-old recovering from a complex diabetic foot ulcer, encountered a sudden barrier to her discharge when the selected home health agency announced a temporary freeze on all new admissions. This administrative delay caused significant distress for her daughter, who was concerned about the gap in professional wound care support once they left the facility. The Navigator served as a steady point of contact, providing education on the Healing Partners transition model and the role of the Medical Assistant, while documenting the family's concerns in Atlantis for the Social Worker to address.
demographic_audit_note: The Navigator performed a thorough audit of the facility face sheet against the Atlantis record and discovered that the patient's primary phone number was transposed. After confirming the correct details with the family at the bedside, the Navigator updated the contact information and verified the home address to ensure the home care team has accurate data for the first visit.
v_card_flyer_status: The Navigator met with Mrs. Rodriguez and her daughter to present the program flyer and successfully integrated the Healing Partners digital contact card into the daughter's phone. The Navigator explained that this would ensure they recognize the caller identification for all future coordination calls from the home team.
pre_dc_pulse_call: During the twenty-four-hour pulse call, the daughter reported that she was still waiting to hear from the nursing agency regarding a specific arrival time. The Navigator documented this continued uncertainty in Atlantis and encouraged the daughter to reach out to the Social Worker for any clinical scheduling updates.
atlantis_final_sync: Following verbal confirmation from the Social Worker that the agency freeze was lifted and the referral was accepted, the Navigator recorded the confirmed discharge date and scheduled the Medical Assistant for the initial home visit within the twenty-four-hour post-discharge window.

format_1_state_log.event_description: The Navigator introduced the Healing Partners program to the patient and updated the Atlantis record with the correct demographic information after finding a discrepancy in the facility face sheet.
The Social Worker informed the Navigator that the preferred home health agency has implemented a temporary intake freeze due to staffing constraints, which will delay the discharge by several days.
The Navigator documented the family's anxiety regarding the intake delay in Atlantis and asked the Social Worker for a status update on when the agency might resume admissions.
The Social Worker confirmed that the agency has reopened intake; the Navigator then scheduled the Medical Assistant visit in Atlantis to follow the patient's return home.

format_2_triples.situation: The family expressed confusion about who would be visiting the home on the first day after discharge.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator provided education on the Healing Partners program and explained the role of the Medical Assistant during the initial twenty-four-hour window.

format_2_triples.situation: The home health agency was unable to process the referral due to an internal administrative freeze.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator noted the delay in Atlantis and alerted the Social Worker to the family's concerns regarding the transition timeline.

format_2_triples.situation: The patient's phone number in the system did not match the information provided by the daughter.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator corrected the demographic data in Atlantis to prevent communication failures during the transition.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator notes that the Social Worker has not yet provided an update on the agency intake freeze, so they document the current status in Atlantis and wait for further instruction from the facility staff.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the boundary that the Social Worker owns the relationship with the home health agency and the discharge timeline.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator asks the Social Worker for a verbal update on the referral status and spends time with the family to explain the Medical Assistant's role and the importance of the program's caller identification.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This action stays within the Navigator's lane by facilitating communication with the internal team and focusing on family education.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator calls the home health agency's intake department directly to ask when the freeze will be lifted so they can provide a firm discharge date to the patient's daughter.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This is an overstep because the Navigator is prohibited from contacting vendors directly, as this role belongs exclusively to the Social Worker.
